pr.title: Split out more stages, split text_2 at top of mpcoll and create text_3
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that split more stage/ground assembly out of monolithic text blobs. It carved new stage files such as grhomerun, grheal, and several grt* target-test stage files, split asm/text_2.s at the top of mpcoll into a new asm/text_3.s, moved the previous later blob onward into asm/text_4.s, and updated obj_files.mk plus ldscript.lcf so the new object order stayed linked in-place.
what_changed: Added new standalone raw assembly files for stage/ground code: grhomerun.s, grheal.s, grtmario.s, grtfalcon.s, grtclink.s, grtdonkey.s, grtdrmario.s, grtfalco.s, and grtfox.s.
Removed 444453 lines from asm/text_2.s and added a new asm/text_3.s containing a large following assembly span; the PR title identifies this as splitting text_2 at the top of mpcoll.
Added asm/text_4.s with 289658 lines, apparently shifting the old later text_3 blob to text_4 after inserting the new text_3 earlier in the object sequence.
Updated obj_files.mk so asm/text_3.o appears immediately after asm/text_2.o, and so the newly split gr*.o files replace the old late text_3.o position before a new asm/text_4.o.
Updated ldscript.lcf FORCEFILES to include grhomerun.o, grheal.o, grtmario.o, grtfalcon.o, grtclink.o, grtdonkey.o, grtdrmario.o, grtfalco.o, and grtfox.o after grlast.o.

The diff excerpt shows the new assembly files use the existing raw asm style: .include "macros.inc", .section .text, address comments, .global func_* labels, lbl_* labels, and SDA/SDA2 label references.
smart_moves: Kept linker ordering explicit by updating both obj_files.mk and ldscript.lcf alongside the file split, which is essential when splitting raw assembly without changing linked addresses.
Inserted the newly created text_3.o near text_2.o and renamed the later blob position to text_4.o instead of only appending files, preserving the intended section sequence.
Split recognizable stage units into gr*.s files, making future C decompilation work more targeted than working inside giant anonymous text_N blobs.
Grouped related target-test stage files together with consistent grt* names, making build order and file discovery easier for later stage decomp work.
Preserved raw function and label names such as func_8021EF38, func_8021EFEC, lbl_804D6AF0, and lbl_804DBC98 instead of guessing semantic names prematurely.
decomp_lessons: For early-stage decomp cleanup, carving monolithic asm/text_N.s files into system-specific raw asm files is useful even before functions are translated to C.
Every assembly split that changes object boundaries must be mirrored in the object list and linker script; missing either file risks address drift or unresolved symbols.
When inserting a new text_N blob in the middle of the link order, renumber or shift subsequent text blobs deliberately and update references in obj_files.mk.
Stage files should be split around natural subsystem boundaries and kept adjacent to neighboring stage objects in link order.
Do not over-name functions during file-splitting PRs when there is no semantic evidence; keep func_* and lbl_* labels until later analysis confirms meaning.
naming_conventions: Ground/stage assembly files use the gr prefix, for example grheal.s and grhomerun.s.
Target-test stage files use grt plus character/stage identifier, for example grtmario.s, grtfalcon.s, grtclink.s, grtdonkey.s, grtdrmario.s, grtfalco.s, and grtfox.s.
Anonymous raw assembly functions retain func_<address> names, for example func_8021EF38 and func_8021EFEC.
Anonymous labels retain lbl_<address> names, including SDA and SDA2 labels such as lbl_804D6AF0-_SDA_BASE_ and lbl_804DBC98-_SDA2_BASE_.
Large leftover raw text blobs are named text_N.s and incremented when new boundaries are inserted.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used raw assembly extraction rather than C translation; new files are one-hunk additions with preserved instruction bytes and address comments.
Maintained existing assembler includes and section declaration: .include "macros.inc" followed by .section .text.
Preserved global function labels and local branch labels exactly, avoiding semantic renaming that could break unresolved references.
Updated FORCEFILES in ldscript.lcf so the newly split objects are forced into the same linker position as their original monolithic assembly.
Updated TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk in the same relative order as the split assembly files, including the new text_3.o and text_4.o positions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no explicit reviewer rationale beyond the merged PR title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that translate grheal.s, grhomerun.s, or grt*.s files from raw asm to C.
Check whether mpcoll boundaries introduced by this text_2/text_3 split were later refined into named source files.
Search for subsequent renumbering or deletion of asm/text_3.s and asm/text_4.s after more stage files were split.
Audit later ldscript.lcf and obj_files.mk changes for the same object order pattern around grlast.o and grhomerun.o.
